codeReview.summary: The feature adds a header theme toggle, early head script, and broad CSS variable migration, but it misses a core requirement: light mode quote/batch/bucket result cards remain dark because the light theme still defines dark result card variables.

issues.description: In the light theme token set (`:root, [data-theme="light"]`), `--result-bg`, `--result-text`, and related result vars are still dark (`#1e1e1e`/`#d4d4d4`). `.result` consumes these vars, so light mode keeps dark result cards, violating the requirement that result cards use a light card style in light mode and that all UI areas are correctly themed.

Several colors remain hardcoded (`color: white;`, e.g., lines 502, 518, 643, 661, 828) instead of custom properties, which does not fully satisfy the explicit requirement to implement theming via CSS custom properties for all colors in `style.css`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no clearly documented convention for representing the 'system' theme state in DOM/localStorage (store `theme=system` vs remove key and set concrete light/dark on `<html>`). This ambiguity leaves dead CSS (`[data-theme="system"]`) and can cause inconsistent implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `static/css/style.css:223` expects `[data-theme="system"]`, but `static/js/app.js:54-58` removes the `theme` key for system mode and sets `data-theme` to `light`/`dark` instead.

summary: Reviewed feature theme-toggle (worker session 614ffc8c-6b9b-4aa7-bd2f-13c96831bab3), including transcript skeleton, handoff, skill, and commit diff for 4e6a047. Marked as fail due to light-mode result cards still using dark styling variables; also noted remaining hardcoded colors and a shared-state convention gap around 'system' theme representation.
